author: @tommywhite3545
text:
Thanks. Nice words.

I have a question I can't seem to find on your channel nor anywhere on the internet. I'm struggling with it. Thats:

What are the electric properties of pristine graphene (so one layer)? 

Especially:
How do I find out or calculate what the storage energy per cm2 (you could call it energy density I guess coz pristine graphene is 2D) and the conductivity of pristine graphene is??

If you could provide me with something I would appreciate it very very much.

author: @michaelshultzinger4902
text:
I don't think I can do it better, what I want is to know where we can get some of these materials in bulk?  I have ideas that aren't material science related but rather building related once I can get my hands on sheets of graphene plastic or graphene reinforced spray foam.  

Any ideas for getting ready to work with materials like what you’ve created?

author: @TheCrochetCase
text: I have to say Robert, I love your videos. So much useful information and inspiration to THINK. As a quirk and a person who loves to know the why behind the 'how' I believe  your channel is perfect. My 10 year old son and I both watch your videos and experiment together. We have had fun with bioplastics, which then lead us to cross polymerisation of silicone with zinc oxide, hydrogen peroxide and glycerine - and a plethora of other interesting and fun lessons. Thanks for lending us your ideas and for helping this nerdy little family find a platform from which to jump.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Wow - that is so cool - I am glad I helped - science rocks and it is so good to hear of  people who want to investigate rather than be pop stars - thanks for sharing this

author: @tonybrooks9324
text: Hi Robert, love your site, makes me wish I had done better at science at school lol.  After watching your post on conductive paint I thought I would give it a go and make some for my electroplating. Not having any joy though !!!  I have been using pure graphite powder mixed with a water based adhesive and also just mixed with water. Any idea where I am going wrong mate ? Any suggestions would be much appreciated, Regards Tony.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the biggest mistake people make is they just don't mix it enough mate - you probably need to buy a muller and retry with that

replies.author: @tonybrooks9324
replies.text: Will keep trying, thanks for the advice and your time :]

author: @BinjKomisar11
text: Thank you! Your channel is one of the most interesting and entertaining wealth of knowledge on youtube. Your self-less sharing of your time and knowledge is a beautiful thing... I cannot wait to really spend some time with the base ideas you have seeded in my mind. Again, thanks Mr Smith.

author: @directeeccks
text: Thanks Robert!  I don't care if people "can do it better".  I honestly can't keep up with the shear amount of information put out on the net.  Your passion and documentation is like an aggregator of information I find useful.  I would have missed some of these things otherwise.  I have found that my personal work has been influenced by some of the things you have presented.  Much respect and cheers!
